name: La Mata and Torrevieja Lagoons Natural Park Loop
name_original: Vuelta completa al Parque Natural de las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja
region: Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja
description: This moderate 26.2-mile mountain bike loop takes you through the unique Parque Natural de las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja.
region_intro: Mountain biking in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja offers routes through a distinctive landscape defined by its two main lagoons: the green Laguna de La Mata and the vibrant pink Laguna de Torrevieja. The terrain is largely semi-arid and salty, supporting unique flora, with areas of vineyards, pine, and eucalyptus forests providing varied scenery. An anticline known as "El Chaparral" separates the lagoons, adding to the geological interest. While generally flat, some trails feature gentle inclines, making the area suitable for various fitness levels. Best mountain bike trails in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja The most popular mountain bike trail is La Mata and Torrevieja Lagoons Natural Park Loop, a 26.2 miles (42.2 km) moderate route that takes about 2 hours 56 minutes to complete, circling both iconic lagoons. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Laguna de La Mata – La Mata Lagoon loop from Área Natural, an easy 14.2 miles (22.8 km) path. This route offers views of the green Laguna de La Mata and its surrounding natural features. Local mountain bikers also love the La Mata Lagoon – Civil War Trenches loop from La Mata, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail leading through the natural park and past historical Civil War trenches, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Mountain biking in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja is defined by its unique pink and green lagoons, semi-arid terrain, and sections of pine and eucalyptus forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes. The routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja?
A: There are over 140 mountain bike trails in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and some more challenging routes to explore the natural park.

Q: What makes the landscape of Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja unique for mountain biking?
A: The park is renowned for its two distinct lagoons: the green &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/864166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de La Mata&lt;/a&gt; and the vibrant pink &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3986767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pink Lake of Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt;, whose color comes from unique algae and bacteria. The terrain is largely semi-arid and salty, featuring unique flora like salicornia and tamarisk, alongside areas of vineyards, pine, and eucalyptus forests. The natural rock formation known as "El Chaparral" also separates the lagoons, adding to the geological interest.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, there are many family-friendly options. The park offers numerous easy routes, perfect for a relaxed ride with children. An excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40386902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Roqueta Beach – Torre de la Mata loop from Vinamar Fase4&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 15.6 km path that provides gentle terrain suitable for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Lagunas De La Mata y Torrevieja Natural Park is generally dog-friendly, but it's important to keep your dog on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially birds, and to respect other visitors. Always check specific trail regulations, as some sensitive areas might have restrictions.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while cycling?
A: The park is a significant wetland and a Special Protection Zone for Birds (ZEPA), making it excellent for birdwatching. You might spot large concentrations of flamingos, black-necked grebes, stilts, common shelducks, and pied avocets, especially near the lagoons and at designated observatories like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5589759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Hut – Parque Natural de las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical points of interest along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the area has historical significance. You can find remnants of Civil War trenches along some routes, adding a unique historical dimension to your ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40391524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata Lagoon – Civil War Trenches loop from La Mata&lt;/a&gt; specifically passes by these historical sites.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the natural park for mountain biking?
A: The natural park offers several parking areas, particularly near the Visitor Center for &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/864166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. These spots provide convenient access to the trailheads. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a space.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja?
A: The best time for mountain biking is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot and dry, making rides less comfortable. The low rainfall and mild temperatures outside of summer create ideal conditions for exploring the trails.

Q: Are there any loop trails that allow me to explore the entire park?
A: Yes, there are comprehensive loop trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930122037/la-mata-and-torrevieja-lagoons-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata and Torrevieja Lagoons Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route, covering 42.2 km and circling both iconic lagoons, offering a full experience of the park's diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails here?
A: The trails in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 250 reviews. Riders often praise the unique scenery of the pink and green lagoons, the well-maintained paths, and the opportunity for peaceful rides through a distinctive natural environment.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike routes suitable for beginners?
A: Absolutely. The park has a good selection of easy routes. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38248834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de La Mata – La Mata Lagoon loop from Área Natural&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 22.8 km path that offers beautiful views of the green Laguna de La Mata with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I access the park using public transport?
A: While direct public transport to specific trailheads within the natural park might be limited, you can typically reach the nearby towns of Torrevieja or La Mata by bus. From there, you might need to cycle or take a short taxi ride to the park's main entrances or visitor centers.
tips: Simple walk, very flat through stony terrain and streams that are easily passable. Along the way there are several viewpoints for bird watching.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Vuelta completa al Parque Natural de las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja?
faqs.answer: The full loop is approximately 42.2 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 57 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This estimate is for riders with good fitness and advanced skills, as the route includes some challenging sections.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. While parts are paved, there are unpaved sections with deeper sand that can be technically challenging, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike. It's not recommended for beginner mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of paved surfaces for a smooth ride and unpaved sections. Be prepared for areas with deeper sand, especially in the unpaved parts, which can be challenging and may require wider tires or walking your bike for short distances. The elevation changes are gentle, with around 120 meters of gain and loss.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and sights along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the unique protected wetlands of the Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja Natural Park. Highlights include cycling alongside the iconic pink-hued Laguna Rosa and the greenish La Mata Lagoon. The route also offers beautiful views, sunny and shady paths, and diverse flora.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The park is a haven for wildlife, particularly birds. You'll have excellent opportunities for bird watching, with flamingos being a particular highlight, especially around the salt lakes. Keep an eye out for other diverse bird species in their natural habitat.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, so early morning rides are advisable if visiting during those months. Winter offers cooler, often sunny conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking areas are available around the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja. While the exact starting point can vary, many riders access the loop from points near the visitor centers or designated parking zones within the park. Look for official park signage for parking options.

faqs.question: Is the Vuelta completa al Parque Natural de las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted in the natural park, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the park or this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is typically no entrance fee or special permit required to access the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja or to ride this specific trail. It is a public natural park, but always respect park regulations and signage.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Vía Verde de Torrevieja, as well as parts of the Guardamar del Segura - Torrevieja route. These intersections can offer options for extending or modifying your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water points along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily passes through natural areas, so facilities like cafes or dedicated water points are limited directly on the route. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of your ride. You may find amenities in nearby towns like Torrevieja or La Mata.
